category_title: Academically gifted students who struggle with an excruciatingly slow reading pace
minimal_context: The student is independently reading a chapter from the novel 'Jeremy Fink and the Meaning of Life' to prepare notes in a booklet for an upcoming literature discussion.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for independent reading. Please read the next chapter of 'Jeremy Fink and the Meaning of Life' and write down your thoughts in your booklet so you are ready for the literature discussion.

STUDENT: (Flipping through the pages to look at the pictures) I hate reading. Reading is boring.

TUTOR: Just focus on the text and keep reading.

STUDENT: (Puts the novel down) My interest has gone down in middle school. I think I need to go spend some time finding a different book.

TUTOR: We are reading this specific novel right now. Please try your best to finish the chapter.

STUDENT: I'm confused by it. I read so slow and get to the point that I barely understand it. Some of the words are hard and I just don't get it.

options.A: Tell the student that finding a different book is a good idea for the reading session.
options.B: Engage the student in 10 minutes of discussion about the chapter after each 10-minute reading session.
options.C: Place a hand on the student's shoulder to redirect his attention back to the novel during the reading session.
options.D: Ignore all of the undesirable behaviors exhibited by the student during the independent reading session.
